articles.title: Man Follows ChatGPT's Advice and Poisons Himself
articles.summary: An older man learned the hard way that ChatGPT isn't to be trusted with health advice after the toxic chatbot landed him in the hospital.
articles.content:
**Man Hospitalized After Following ChatGPT’s Dangerous Health Advice**  
*AI misuse highlights the risks of relying on artificial intelligence for personal health decisions.*  

In a concerning incident that underscores the dangers of uncritically trusting artificial intelligence, an elderly man was hospitalized after following medical advice provided by ChatGPT, OpenAI’s popular chatbot. The mishap occurred when the man, whose name has not been disclosed for privacy reasons, sought help for a persistent health issue but unwittingly followed guidance that led to self-poisoning.  

According to medical professionals at the hospital where the man was treated, he arrived in critical condition after ingesting a substance that the chatbot had suggested as a home remedy. While the exact substance hasn’t been revealed, sources familiar with the incident say it was a common household product that, in certain doses, can be highly toxic.  

### A Dangerous Reliance on AI  
The man, described by family members as “resourceful but not tech-savvy,” reportedly turned to ChatGPT after struggling to get a timely appointment with his primary care physician. He asked the AI chatbot for suggestions to relieve his symptoms and received advice that sounded plausible but was ultimately hazardous.  

“He thought he was getting accurate medical guidance,” said the man’s daughter, who found him unresponsive and called emergency services. “He had no idea the advice was coming from a chatbot that has no real medical expertise.”  

ChatGPT, which is designed to generate human-like text based on prompts, explicitly states that it is not a substitute for professional medical advice. However, some users, either due to misunderstanding or desperation, continue to treat the AI as a reliable source for health-related information.  

### OpenAI’s Warning Labels Ignored  
OpenAI, the company behind ChatGPT, has implemented disclaimers and safeguards to prevent misuse. The chatbot is programmed to include warnings in its responses, such as advising users to consult qualified medical professionals. However, these warnings can sometimes be overlooked or dismissed, especially among individuals who may not fully understand the limitations of artificial intelligence.  

In this particular case, the chatbot reportedly provided a disclaimer, but the man either missed it or chose to proceed anyway. “It’s a reminder that these tools are not foolproof, and they should never replace expert advice,” said Dr. Emily Carter, an emergency physician who treated the patient.  

### The Growing Risks of AI Misuse  
This incident adds to a growing list of examples where AI tools have been misused, often with alarming consequences. While ChatGPT and similar systems have proven useful in assisting with creative writing, coding, and general knowledge queries, their limitations become apparent when users seek precise or high-stakes advice.  

“People need to understand that these models are trained on a vast amount of data, but they don’t actually ‘know’ anything,” said Dr. Rajesh Patel, a technology ethics expert. “They mimic human language, but they don’t verify facts in real time. They can’t predict the consequences of their advice.”  

Experts are now calling for stricter regulations and clearer public education about the appropriate uses of AI. “It’s not enough to rely on disclaimers buried in the fine print,” Patel added. “We need to ensure that people understand the risks upfront.”  

### A Lesson for Users  
Fortunately, the man is expected to recover fully after receiving prompt medical treatment. However, his family hopes the ordeal will serve as a cautionary tale for others.  

“I just want people to know that you can’t trust everything you read, especially when it comes from something like ChatGPT,” his daughter said. “It’s a tool, not a doctor.”  

As AI continues to evolve and integrate into daily life, this incident is a stark reminder of the importance of using such technology responsibly. For those seeking medical advice or facing health concerns, experts universally agree: when in doubt, consult a qualified healthcare professional—not a chatbot.

articles.title: Malaria Cases in Delhi Hit a 5-Year High As Dengue and Chikungunya Spread Surge, Know Preventive Tips
articles.summary: Delhi sees malaria cases at a 5-year high amid rising dengue and chikungunya infections. Learn key preventive tips to protect yourself from mosquito-borne diseases this monsoon season. TheHealthSite.com
articles.content:
**Malaria Cases in Delhi Hit a 5-Year High As Dengue and Chikungunya Spread Surge: Know Preventive Tips**  

*Delhi grapples with a surge in mosquito-borne diseases as authorities urge residents to take proactive measures to curb infections.*  

**New Delhi:** In a worrying trend, malaria cases in Delhi have soared to a five-year high, raising alarm bells across the capital. Alongside malaria, the city is also witnessing an uptick in dengue and chikungunya infections, heightening concerns as the monsoon season creates the perfect breeding grounds for disease-carrying mosquitoes.  

According to data released by the municipal health department, malaria cases have been steadily climbing, with over 800 confirmed cases reported in the city this year alone—the highest tally since 2018. Meanwhile, dengue infections have surged past 1,200 cases, and chikungunya, though less prevalent, has recorded over 200 cases. Experts attribute the spike to prolonged monsoon rains, waterlogging, and inconsistent mosquito control measures.  

**The Seasonal Surge**  
The monsoon season, which brings much-needed relief from scorching summer temperatures, also ushers in stagnant water pools ideal for mosquitoes to breed. Construction sites, uncovered drains, discarded containers, and unmaintained water tanks have become hotspots for mosquito proliferation across urban and suburban areas.  

Dr. Rajesh Kumar, a public health expert, explained, "Monsoon rains exacerbate the mosquito menace. Combined with improper water management and lack of public awareness, mosquito-borne diseases like malaria, dengue, and chikungunya thrive during this time."  

Delhi is not alone in facing this challenge. Other cities across northern India have reported rising mosquito-borne infections, prompting health officials to issue advisories and ramp up fumigation drives.  

**Symptoms to Watch For**  
Malaria, caused by the Plasmodium parasite and transmitted through infected Anopheles mosquitoes, presents symptoms such as fever, chills, headache, and nausea. Dengue, known for its severe flu-like symptoms, including high fever, joint pain, and skin rashes, can sometimes lead to life-threatening complications like dengue hemorrhagic fever. Chikungunya, transmitted by Aedes mosquitoes, often results in debilitating joint pain, fever, and skin rashes.  

Residents are advised to seek immediate medical attention if they experience any of these symptoms, as early diagnosis and treatment can prevent complications.  

**Preventive Measures to Protect Yourself**  
Amid the surge, health experts and government authorities are emphasizing preventive measures to curb the spread of mosquito-borne diseases. Here are some key tips to protect yourself during this season:  

1. **Eliminate Standing Water:** Regularly check your surroundings for stagnant water in pots, drains, discarded tires, and containers, as these are prime breeding grounds for mosquitoes.  

2. **Use Mosquito Repellents:** Apply mosquito repellent creams or sprays, especially when outdoors. Opt for natural alternatives like citronella oil if you prefer chemical-free solutions.  

3. **Install Mosquito Nets:** Ensure your windows and doors are fitted with wire mesh to keep mosquitoes out. Sleeping under mosquito nets can provide added protection.  

4. **Wear Protective Clothing:** Cover as much skin as possible by wearing long-sleeved clothing and full-length pants, particularly during dusk and dawn when mosquitoes are most active.  

5. **Fumigation and Cleanliness Drives:** Encourage local communities to participate in fumigation campaigns and maintain cleanliness to reduce mosquito populations in shared spaces.  

6. **Stay Hydrated and Boost Immunity:** A healthy immune system can help your body fight infections. Include nutrient-rich foods in your diet and drink plenty of water.  

**Government and Community Action**  
The Delhi government has ramped up anti-mosquito measures, including intensified fumigation drives, public awareness campaigns, and increased surveillance for mosquito larvae in waterlogged areas. However, authorities stress that community involvement is critical to controlling the spread.  

Residents are urged to report mosquito breeding sites and actively participate in neighborhood cleanliness initiatives. Schools and workplaces have also been advised to promote preventive practices like wearing protective clothing and using mosquito repellents.  

**The Bigger Picture**  
The spike in mosquito-borne diseases serves as a stark reminder of the importance of sustainable urban planning and public health infrastructure. Experts argue that long-term solutions, such as improved drainage systems and regular sanitation drives, are essential to preventing annual surges in infections.  

As Delhi residents brace for the remainder of the monsoon season, vigilance and preventive measures will be key to safeguarding public health. By working collaboratively, the city can overcome this seasonal challenge and ensure healthier living conditions for all.

articles.title: I Became a First-Time Mom at 45 and Love Being an Older Parent
articles.summary: After 10 years of trying IVF, I suddenly got pregnant naturally at 46. I was worried about being an older mom, but I realized it has many benefits.
articles.content:
**I Became a First-Time Mom at 45 and Love Being an Older Parent**  
*By [Your Name]*  

For most of my adult life, I had envisioned becoming a mother. I pictured bedtime stories, messy art projects, and the tiny shoes scattered across the floor. But as the years slipped by, my dream seemed increasingly distant. After a decade of trying in vitro fertilization (IVF) with no success, I began to wonder if it was time to let go of that hope. Then, at the age of 46, something extraordinary happened—I got pregnant naturally.  

It was a moment of disbelief and overwhelming joy. My husband and I had long since stopped expecting positive results from pregnancy tests. The sight of those two pink lines felt surreal. But with the excitement came an onslaught of questions and fears. Could my body handle the demands of pregnancy at this age? Would I have enough energy to keep up with a baby? And how would society perceive me as an older mom?  

Nine months later, I held my beautiful baby girl in my arms. She was everything I had dreamed of and more. And while there have been challenges, becoming a first-time mom at 45 has been one of the most rewarding experiences of my life.  

### The Challenges of Late Motherhood  

There’s no denying that being an older mom comes with its own set of challenges. Pregnancy itself was harder on my body than it might have been in my 20s or 30s. I experienced fatigue and mild complications, and I was acutely aware of the increased risks associated with advanced maternal age, such as gestational diabetes and preeclampsia.  

As I adjusted to life with a newborn, I faced new hurdles. Sleepless nights took a toll, and I occasionally doubted my stamina. At times, I worried about how my age might impact my daughter in the future—would I be there for her major milestones? Would I be the "old mom" at school events?  

But for every challenge, there has been an even greater joy. And what I didn’t anticipate were the unexpected benefits of parenting later in life.  

### The Gifts of Being an Older Parent  

One of the greatest advantages of becoming a mom at 45 is the perspective and patience that come with age. In my 20s and 30s, I was consumed by building my career and figuring out who I was. By the time my daughter arrived, I felt more secure in myself and my priorities. I had the wisdom to let go of perfection and focus on what truly matters.  

I also feel incredibly grateful in a way that might not have been possible in my younger years. After so many years of longing for motherhood, I don’t take a single moment for granted. Whether it’s the sound of her giggles or the quiet moments before bedtime, I savor it all.  

Financial stability is another major plus. By the time I became a parent, my husband and I had built a solid foundation. We’re able to provide for our daughter without the financial stress that can often accompany parenting at a younger age.  

And as for being the "old mom"—I’ve learned to embrace it. I’ve found a wonderful community of parents of all ages, and I’ve realized that love transcends numbers. My daughter doesn’t see me as older; she just sees me as Mom.  

### Looking Ahead  

Becoming a first-time mom at 45 has taught me that there’s no "perfect" timeline for parenthood. Everyone’s journey looks different, and that’s okay. While my path to motherhood was longer and more winding than I expected, it was worth every step.  

My daughter has brought a sense of purpose and joy to my life that I never could have imagined. I may not have the same energy I did in my 20s, but I have something even more valuable: a deep appreciation for this incredible chapter of life.  

To anyone who’s considering parenthood later in life, I want to say this: It’s never too late to follow your dreams. Motherhood is a journey filled with surprises, challenges, and unending love—and it’s worth every single moment.  

As I watch my little girl take her first steps and say her first words, I’m reminded of how lucky I am to be her mom. And while I may have started this journey a bit later than most, I wouldn’t change a thing.

articles.title: Psychotherapy for Chronic Dizziness: A Scoping Review
articles.summary:
Chronic dizziness is a frequent condition that substantially impairs quality of life (QoL), making psychotherapy a crucial intervention alongside physical therapies. However, the overall landscape of evidence regarding its application to diverse dizziness disorders remains unclear. This scoping review aimed to comprehensively map the existing research on this topic, providing an overview of its utilization, intervention methods, and evaluation measures to identify research gaps. Following the Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic reviews and Meta-Analyses extension for Scoping Reviews (PRISMA-ScR) guidelines, we searched PubMed and CiNii Research for peer-reviewed articles published in English or Japanese. Eligible studies were those describing a psychotherapeutic intervention for patients with chronic dizziness lasting three months or longer, published up to September 30, 2023. A total of 644 records were screened, with 23 studies ultimately included for analysis.

Our results indicate that the current evidence base is largely developmental. The most common study design was the case study (10/23 studies, 43.5%), with a median of seven participants. The median age of participants was 45.2 years, indicating a primary focus on adult populations. The predominant psychotherapeutic technique was cognitive behavioral therapy (16/23 studies, 69.6%), typically delivered individually, with a median of 6.5 sessions. While evaluations were often multifaceted, assessing domains such as dizziness symptoms, psychological state, QoL, and physical function, the outcome measures used were highly diverse and not standardized. The Dizziness Handicap Inventory (DHI) and the Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale (HADS) were the most frequently used instruments. Although the included studies collectively suggest that psychotherapy is a promising intervention for improving symptoms of chronic dizziness, this review highlights several key limitations of the existing evidence base: (a) it consists primarily of small-scale case studies, with a lack of high-quality evidence; (b) the research focuses on adult populations, with a scarcity of studies targeting children or adolescents; (c) while group formats and combination therapies show potential, the research is too limited to draw firm conclusions; and (d) the outcome measures used are highly diverse and not standardized. Therefore, to advance the field, future research must prioritize conducting high-quality intervention studies, particularly large-scale RCTs, that utilize standardized outcome measures.
articles.content:
**Psychotherapy for Chronic Dizziness: A Scoping Review Reveals Promising Potential and Research Gaps**

*October 2023 — Chronic dizziness, a condition that significantly impairs quality of life, continues to challenge patients and healthcare providers alike. Often described as a persistent sense of imbalance, spinning, or lightheadedness, chronic dizziness affects millions worldwide and can last for months or even years, severely impacting physical, psychological, and social functioning. While physical therapies remain a cornerstone of treatment, psychotherapy is gaining recognition as a complementary intervention. However, the overall landscape of evidence supporting psychotherapeutic approaches has remained unclear—until now.*

A new scoping review published this month aims to provide clarity by mapping the existing research on psychotherapy for chronic dizziness. The review, conducted in accordance with the rigorous Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses extension for Scoping Reviews (PRISMA-ScR) guidelines, analyzes studies published in English and Japanese up to September 30, 2023. Researchers screened a total of 644 records, ultimately identifying 23 studies that met their inclusion criteria.

### **Unveiling the Evidence: Key Findings**

The findings paint a picture of a developing field with promising potential but significant gaps:

1. **Study Designs and Participant Demographics**  
   The majority of studies included were small-scale case studies, accounting for 43.5% of the evidence base (10 out of 23 studies). The median number of participants was just seven, highlighting the limited scope of existing research. Most studies focused on adult populations, with a median participant age of 45.2 years, leaving questions about how psychotherapy might benefit children and adolescents with chronic dizziness.

2. **Therapeutic Methods and Delivery**  
   Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) emerged as the predominant psychotherapeutic technique, used in 69.6% of studies (16 out of 23). Typically delivered individually, CBT interventions involved a median of 6.5 sessions. While group therapy formats and combination approaches combining psychotherapy with physical therapies showed potential, the evidence remains too sparse to draw definitive conclusions.

3. **Outcome Measures: Diverse and Non-Standardized**  
   Evaluations of psychotherapeutic interventions often assessed multiple domains, including dizziness symptoms, psychological state, quality of life (QoL), and physical function. However, the instruments used to measure outcomes varied widely, complicating comparisons across studies. The Dizziness Handicap Inventory (DHI) and the Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale (HADS) were the most frequently used tools, but the lack of standardization underscores the need for a unified approach to evaluation.

### **The Promise of Psychotherapy**

Despite these limitations, the review highlights the promise of psychotherapy as a valuable intervention for chronic dizziness. The studies collectively suggest that psychotherapeutic approaches can improve dizziness symptoms, alleviate psychological distress, and enhance overall QoL. CBT, in particular, has demonstrated the potential to address the anxiety and depressive symptoms that often accompany chronic dizziness, creating a pathway to better coping strategies and reduced symptom severity.

### **Challenges and Research Gaps**

While the findings are encouraging, the authors of the review identify several critical limitations that must be addressed to advance the field:

- **Small Sample Sizes and Lack of Robust Evidence**  
  The reliance on small-scale case studies limits the generalizability of findings, underscoring the need for larger, high-quality randomized controlled trials (RCTs).

- **Adult-Centric Research**  
  The scarcity of studies targeting children and adolescents leaves a significant gap in understanding how psychotherapy might benefit younger populations.

- **Unexplored Formats and Combinations**  
  Group therapy formats and combination interventions, though promising, require further exploration through well-designed studies.

- **Non-Standardized Outcome Measures**  
  The diversity of evaluation tools complicates efforts to compare and synthesize findings. Establishing standardized measures would enhance the reliability and comparability of future research.

### **Looking Ahead: Priorities for Future Research**

The scoping review concludes with a call to action for researchers and clinicians. To establish psychotherapy as an evidence-based intervention for chronic dizziness, future studies must prioritize:

- Conducting large-scale RCTs with diverse participant samples to strengthen the evidence base.
- Investigating the efficacy of psychotherapeutic interventions in pediatric populations.
- Exploring innovative delivery methods, such as group therapy and technology-assisted formats.
- Standardizing outcome measures to improve consistency and comparability across studies.

### **Why It Matters**

Chronic dizziness is more than just a physical condition—it is a deeply disruptive force that impacts every aspect of daily life. Patients often face challenges in maintaining employment, engaging in social activities, and managing their mental health. By expanding the evidence base for psychotherapy, healthcare providers can offer more comprehensive and effective care, addressing both the physical and psychological dimensions of this condition.

As the field of psychotherapy for chronic dizziness continues to evolve, this scoping review serves as a critical roadmap, shedding

articles.title: Elderly people being excluded from medical research in UK, charities warn
articles.summary: The exclusion of older people from research due to arbitrary age cut-offs could stall the development of life-saving treatments
articles.content:
**Elderly People Being Excluded from Medical Research in the UK, Charities Warn**

In a troubling revelation, leading health charities have raised the alarm over the exclusion of elderly people from medical research in the United Kingdom. This practice, driven by arbitrary age cut-offs, is not only discriminatory but could have far-reaching consequences for the development of life-saving treatments, they warn.

Aging populations are a defining characteristic of modern society, with the UK’s Office for National Statistics projecting that nearly a quarter of the population will be aged 65 or older by 2043. Yet, despite their growing numbers, older adults remain significantly underrepresented in clinical trials and biomedical research. 

“Older people are often excluded from trials based on outdated assumptions about age or the mistaken belief that their participation introduces too many variables,” said Dr. Amelia Clarke, a spokesperson for Age UK, one of the charities advocating for change. “This is not only unfair—it’s unscientific. Older people are the primary users of many treatments being developed, so their exclusion undermines the very purpose of medical research.”

### The Problem with Age Cut-Offs

Age cut-offs in research protocols often stem from concerns about the potential complexity of studying older adults. This demographic is more likely to have multiple health conditions, take multiple medications, or experience age-related changes in metabolism that might affect how treatments work. However, experts argue that such factors are precisely why older people *should* be included in research—not excluded from it.

“Medicine isn’t one-size-fits-all,” said Dr. Ravi Patel, a geriatrician at King’s College Hospital. “We need to understand how treatments work in the populations most likely to use them. Ignoring older adults in research means we lack critical data to provide them with safe and effective care.”

The consequences of this exclusion are stark. For instance, older patients often receive medications or therapies based on data collected from much younger participants, which can render treatments less effective—or even harmful—for them. In fields such as oncology, cardiology, and neurology, where age is a significant risk factor, this data gap is particularly troubling.

### A Barrier to Innovation

The exclusion of older adults doesn’t just affect them as individuals; it also hampers scientific progress and innovation. According to the charities, this practice could stall the development of breakthrough treatments for age-related diseases such as dementia, arthritis, and osteoporosis, as well as chronic conditions like diabetes and heart disease.

“By excluding older adults, we’re essentially ignoring the realities of aging,” said Professor Linda Hartwell, a specialist in clinical research ethics. “This not only delays progress in treating these diseases but also limits our understanding of how to improve quality of life in later years.”

### Calls for Change

In response to these concerns, charities including Age UK, Alzheimer’s Society, and the British Geriatrics Society are calling for an overhaul of research practices. They are urging funding bodies, regulatory agencies, and research institutions to eliminate arbitrary age restrictions and adopt age-inclusive study designs.

The National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR) has already taken steps in this direction, issuing guidance to ensure that older people are adequately represented in studies it funds. However, campaigners say more needs to be done across the board, particularly by pharmaceutical companies and private research organizations.

Public awareness is also a crucial part of the solution, according to campaigners. They are encouraging older people to ask their healthcare providers about opportunities to participate in research and to advocate for their inclusion in studies.

### A Path Forward

As the UK grapples with an aging population and the rising demand for age-appropriate healthcare, the need for change has never been more urgent. Including older adults in medical research isn’t just a matter of equity—it’s a scientific imperative.

“We owe it to older generations to ensure that medical advances benefit everyone, not just the young and healthy,” said Dr. Clarke. “Their participation in research isn’t a liability; it’s an asset. It’s time to recognize that and act accordingly.”

The charities’ call to action is a reminder that advancing medical science requires inclusivity at every level. By addressing this issue, the UK has an opportunity to set a global standard for ethical and effective research—one that prioritizes the needs of all, regardless of age.

articles.title: Seven daily habits to control, destroy cancer cells: Expert explains
articles.summary: Ketogenic foods that are high in fats and low in carbs can prevent the cancer cells from growing at a rapid pace..cancer prevention tips, cancer control habits, healthy lifestyle for cancer, cancer and diet, stress and cancer, sugar intake and cancer, ketogenic diet for cancer, exercise and cancer, intermittent fasting for cancer, inflammation and cancer, cancer research, Dr. Thomas N Seyfried, cancer wardile chiri, Innocent Malayalam actor, Malayala Manorama Online News
articles.content:
**Seven Daily Habits to Control, Destroy Cancer Cells: Expert Explains**

*By [Your Name]*

In the fight against cancer, lifestyle choices can play a pivotal role in prevention and management. Dr. Thomas N. Seyfried, a renowned cancer researcher and advocate for metabolic therapies, underscores the importance of daily habits that can help control and destroy cancer cells. From dietary changes to stress management, these strategies are rooted in cutting-edge research aimed at empowering individuals to take charge of their health.

### The Ketogenic Diet: Starving Cancer Cells
One of the standout recommendations from Dr. Seyfried is adopting a ketogenic diet, which is high in healthy fats and low in carbohydrates. Cancer cells thrive on glucose for energy, and by limiting carb intake, the body enters a state of ketosis, using fat as its primary fuel source. This metabolic shift can prevent cancer cells from growing at a rapid pace. Foods like avocados, nuts, seeds, olive oil, and fatty fish are staples in this diet, offering both nourishment and a strategic defense against cancer.

### Sugar: The Silent Culprit
Recent studies have highlighted the dangerous relationship between sugar and cancer. Excessive sugar intake not only fuels cancer cells but also promotes inflammation, which is linked to the development of various diseases. Dr. Seyfried advises reducing refined sugars and processed foods in favor of whole, nutrient-dense options to minimize cancer risk.

### Intermittent Fasting: Giving Cells a Break
Intermittent fasting is another effective habit that can help in controlling cancer. By limiting eating windows, the body experiences periods of low insulin levels, which can inhibit the growth of cancer cells. This approach also encourages autophagy—a natural process where damaged or unhealthy cells are broken down and recycled, reducing cancer risk.

### Exercise: Moving Towards Prevention
Physical activity plays a critical role in cancer prevention. Regular exercise improves immune function, reduces inflammation, and helps regulate hormones that may contribute to cancer growth. Dr. Seyfried recommends incorporating moderate exercise like walking, cycling, or yoga into daily routines to enhance overall health and resilience.

### Stress Management: A Hidden Factor
Chronic stress can have devastating effects on the body, including increasing the risk of cancer. High stress levels lead to the release of cortisol, which can suppress the immune system and promote inflammation. Mindfulness practices such as meditation, deep breathing, and spending time in nature are simple yet powerful ways to counteract the harmful effects of stress.

### Anti-Inflammatory Foods: Healing from Within
Inflammation is a key driver of many chronic illnesses, including cancer. Incorporating anti-inflammatory foods like turmeric, ginger, berries, and leafy greens into your diet can help reduce inflammation and strengthen the body’s ability to fight off disease.

### Research and Hope
Dr. Seyfried's groundbreaking work in cancer research highlights the importance of metabolic health in combating this disease. His findings provide hope for individuals seeking proactive ways to manage their risk. While cancer is a complex and multifaceted illness, adopting these habits can significantly improve overall health and contribute to a longer, healthier life.

### A Local Connection
In Kerala, discussions about cancer prevention have gained traction following the tragic loss of Innocent, a beloved Malayalam actor, to the disease. His passing has stirred conversations around adopting healthier lifestyles, with many turning to resources like *Malayala Manorama Online* for guidance. The culture of Kerala, rich in natural produce and traditional cooking, offers an excellent foundation for embracing these habits.

### The Road Ahead
While no single habit can guarantee immunity from cancer, combining these strategies into a daily routine can create a strong defense against the disease. Dr. Seyfried’s insights remind us that prevention is often the best cure. By prioritizing a healthy lifestyle, we can take meaningful steps toward reducing cancer risk and improving overall well-being.

For those seeking more information, resources on cancer prevention and management are widely available, including expert recommendations, dietary plans, and support networks. Taking small, consistent steps today could make all the difference tomorrow.

---

articles.title: I thought we’d entered the age of body positivity. Then came ‘shrinking girl summer’ - is everyone getting smaller except me?
articles.summary: It’s been the year of weight-loss drugs, with celebrities seemingly disappearing before our eyes. For those of us left behind, it’s both a torment and a temptation. Spoiler alert: I tried the jabs, too
articles.content:
**I Thought We’d Entered the Age of Body Positivity. Then Came ‘Shrinking Girl Summer’—Is Everyone Getting Smaller Except Me?**

For years, the body positivity movement was touted as a cultural turning point, a long-overdue embrace of all shapes and sizes in a world that had long glorified only the slim and toned. Social media feeds were awash with empowering hashtags like #BodyPositivity and #AllBodiesAreBeautiful. Brands diversified their marketing campaigns, featuring models of every size, and the public conversation shifted—at least on the surface. But as summer arrived this year, something else began to take shape, or perhaps un-shape. 

Welcome to “Shrinking Girl Summer,” a season where it feels as though everyone—especially celebrities—is disappearing before our eyes. The culprit? A new class of weight-loss drugs, originally developed to treat Type 2 diabetes, that has rapidly become the latest cultural phenomenon. For those of us still grounded in the reality of our unchanging bodies, it’s both a torment and a temptation.

### A New Type of Transformation

The buzz around drugs like Ozempic and Wegovy skyrocketed over the past year. These injectable medications, which work by suppressing appetite and regulating blood sugar, have been nothing short of revolutionary for many with medical needs. But their off-label use for rapid weight loss has catapulted them into a different kind of spotlight—one where Hollywood elites, influencers, and even your coworker’s best friend are swapping before-and-after stories like trading cards. 

By the time summer rolled around, it wasn’t just a whisper anymore. The evidence was everywhere: red carpet appearances, Instagram selfies, and gossip columns speculating who was using “the jabs.” A-listers who once championed body positivity were now appearing visibly slimmer, their newly chiselled jawlines and lithe frames sparking a mixture of admiration and unease.

### Watching From the Sidelines

For those of us who’ve spent years cultivating self-acceptance, the rise of these weight-loss drugs feels like a betrayal. I, for one, believed we had collectively moved past the obsession with shrinking ourselves to fit an impossible standard. Yet, as I scroll through photos of celebrities looking slimmer than ever, a nagging thought creeps in: is everyone getting smaller except me?

The irony is hard to ignore. After years of fighting for inclusivity and promoting the idea that all bodies are worthy, we’re now witnessing a cultural shift that feels eerily like a return to the thin-obsessed 1990s. Only now, instead of crash diets and thigh-master workouts, the transformation is pharmaceutical, quick, and—at least to some—irresistible.

### The Torment and the Temptation

I’ll admit it: I’ve been tempted. The speed and seeming ease of these transformations are tantalizing. Who wouldn’t want the ability to bypass years of diet culture baggage with a simple injection? But there’s also the torment—the guilt of wanting to conform to a standard I’ve spent years rejecting.

Spoiler alert: I tried the jabs, too. I justified it to myself as “curiosity” and “research.” But deep down, I knew it wasn’t just that. I wanted to see if I could be one of the shrinking girls, too. And for a brief moment, I was. The scale moved, and my clothes fit differently. But what I didn’t anticipate was the emotional toll. The process brought up insecurities I thought I’d buried long ago, reigniting my fraught relationship with food, weight, and self-worth.

### The Bigger Picture

It’s easy to blame the weight-loss drug craze for undoing years of progress, but the truth is more complicated. The body positivity movement has always coexisted uneasily with a culture that prizes thinness above all else. These drugs didn’t create that culture; they simply exposed how deeply ingrained it still is.

What’s troubling, though, is how quickly the narrative has shifted. The very celebrities who once championed self-love and acceptance are the same ones now showcasing dramatic transformations. For the average person—especially those of us already struggling to feel comfortable in our skin—it sends a clear, if unspoken, message: body positivity is great, but thinness is still the ultimate goal.

### Where Do We Go From Here?

As “Shrinking Girl Summer” fades into fall, I can’t help but wonder what comes next. Will we continue down this path, chasing a pharmaceutical shortcut to an ideal that’s always just out of reach? Or will we finally reckon with the contradictions and double standards that have long defined our relationship with weight and beauty?

I don’t have the answers, but I do know this: my worth isn’t determined by a number on the scale or the size of my jeans. And while the temptation of “shrinking” may still linger, I

articles.title: Is your liver at risk? Doctors explain the top 3 causes of liver damage and how you can prevent it
articles.summary: Certain lifestyle habits put your liver health at risk, affecting its ability to carry out its functions like filtering toxins and supporting metabolism.  | Health
articles.content:
**Is Your Liver at Risk? Doctors Explain the Top 3 Causes of Liver Damage and How You Can Prevent It**

The liver is a silent workhorse, tirelessly performing over 500 critical functions to keep your body healthy. From filtering toxins out of your bloodstream to aiding digestion and regulating metabolism, this vital organ often goes unappreciated—until something goes wrong. Unfortunately, liver damage is becoming increasingly common, and many people are unaware that their daily habits could be putting this essential organ at risk.

To shed light on the issue, health experts are raising awareness about the top three causes of liver damage and offering practical tips for prevention.

---

### **1. Excessive Alcohol Consumption**
It’s no secret that alcohol can harm your liver. Chronic alcohol use is one of the leading causes of liver disease worldwide, potentially leading to conditions like fatty liver disease, alcoholic hepatitis, and even cirrhosis—a severe form of liver scarring. 

Dr. Melissa Carter, a hepatologist at New York General Hospital, explains, “Your liver metabolizes alcohol to detoxify your body, but excessive consumption overwhelms this process, leading to inflammation and long-term damage. The risk increases with the amount and frequency of alcohol intake.”

**Prevention Tip**: Moderation is key. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) recommends limiting alcohol consumption to one drink per day for women and up to two drinks per day for men. If you suspect your drinking habits may be problematic, seek professional help early to avoid irreversible liver damage.

---

### **2. Poor Diet and Obesity**
The modern diet, often high in processed foods, added sugars, and unhealthy fats, is fueling a surge in non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD). This condition occurs when excess fat builds up in liver cells, impairing its function. Left untreated, NAFLD can progress to more severe forms of liver disease, such as non-alcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH) and cirrhosis.

“Obesity and poor dietary choices are major contributors to fatty liver disease,” says Dr. Raj Patel, a gastroenterologist specializing in liver health. “The liver is not designed to store large amounts of fat. Over time, this can lead to inflammation, scarring, and eventually liver failure.”

**Prevention Tip**: Adopting healthier eating habits is crucial. Focus on a balanced diet rich in whole foods, such as fruits, vegetables, lean proteins, and whole grains. Regular exercise can also help you maintain a healthy weight and reduce fat buildup in the liver.

---

### **3. Viral Hepatitis**
Viral hepatitis, particularly hepatitis B and C, is another major culprit behind liver damage. These infections can cause chronic inflammation, leading to liver fibrosis, cirrhosis, and even liver cancer if left untreated. According to the World Health Organization (WHO), millions of people worldwide live with hepatitis B or C, and many are unaware they are infected.

“The danger with hepatitis is that it’s often a silent disease in its early stages,” warns Dr. Emily Nguyen, an infectious disease specialist. “Symptoms may not appear until significant liver damage has occurred, which is why screening and vaccination are so important.”

**Prevention Tip**: Vaccination is available for hepatitis B, and early detection is possible for hepatitis C through routine blood tests. Regular check-ups with your healthcare provider, especially if you are at higher risk due to factors like unprotected sex, shared needles, or a family history of the disease, can help catch infections early.

---

### **Why Your Liver Deserves Your Attention**
The liver is unique in its ability to regenerate, but it’s not invincible. Once significant damage occurs, the road to recovery can be long and challenging. The good news is that many liver problems are preventable by making healthier lifestyle choices and seeking medical care when needed.

“Taking care of your liver is an investment in your overall health,” says Dr. Carter. “It’s never too late to make changes that will protect this vital organ.”

---

### **Key Takeaways for a Healthier Liver**
- Limit alcohol consumption to recommended guidelines.
- Maintain a healthy weight through a balanced diet and regular exercise.
- Stay informed about viral hepatitis and get vaccinated or screened as appropriate.
- Avoid risky behaviors, such as sharing needles or engaging in unprotected sex, that can increase your risk of hepatitis.
- Consult your doctor if you have concerns about your liver health.

Your liver is a vital ally in your body’s daily functions, and it deserves your care and attention. By understanding the risks and taking proactive steps, you can ensure that this hardworking organ continues to serve you well for years to come.

articles.title: Innovative Surgical Management of Nasal Septal Lipoma: A Report of a Rare Case and Review of Literature
articles.summary:
Nasal septal lipoma is a very rare entity that is encountered in otolaryngology, head and neck surgery, with only 16 reported cases in the literature, to the best of our knowledge. We have reported a child with an isolated septal lipoma without any associated syndromes or intracranial extension. 

Surgical excision was done, and the lipoma was found to be adherent to the nasal mucosa, so complete excision of the mass with its nasal mucosa was performed, and reconstruction of the defect was done using a cartilage-skin composite graft of the involved area. To our knowledge, this is the first case to be reconstructed using this technique and with complete healing of the nasal mucosa. A comprehensive review of the reported cases is done in this case report.
articles.content:
**Innovative Surgical Management of Nasal Septal Lipoma: A Rare Case Breaks New Ground**

In a groundbreaking advancement in otolaryngology, a rare case of nasal septal lipoma in a child has been successfully treated using an innovative surgical approach. Nasal septal lipomas are exceedingly rare, with only 16 cases documented in medical literature worldwide. This latest case not only adds to the limited pool of knowledge about this condition but also introduces a pioneering surgical technique that could pave the way for future treatments.

### A Rare Diagnosis

Lipomas, benign tumors composed of adipose (fat) tissue, are commonly found in various parts of the body, but their occurrence in the nasal septum is exceptionally unusual. Nasal septal lipomas are not typically linked to syndromes or intracranial extensions, making them an isolated anomaly. In this case, a young patient presented with an isolated nasal septal lipoma, which posed unique challenges for surgical management and reconstruction.

The patient, whose identity remains confidential for privacy reasons, exhibited no associated syndromes or complications, such as intracranial extensions. This allowed the surgical team to focus solely on the nasal septal region, providing an opportunity for innovation in both excision and reconstruction techniques.

### Surgical Innovation

The surgical team undertook a meticulous excision of the lipoma, which was found to be adherent to the nasal mucosa—a layer of tissue lining the nasal cavity. This adhesion complicated the removal process, as preserving the structural and functional integrity of the nasal septum was paramount.

To ensure complete removal of the tumor while minimizing potential complications, the team opted for a full excision of both the lipoma and the involved nasal mucosa. What makes this case remarkable is the reconstruction method employed to address the resulting defect. Using a cartilage-skin composite graft, the surgeons successfully reconstructed the area, restoring both the functionality and appearance of the nasal septum.

This technique marks the first reported instance of a nasal septal defect being reconstructed using a cartilage-skin composite graft. The innovative approach demonstrated impressive results, with the nasal mucosa achieving complete healing post-surgery.

### A Promising Outcome

Postoperative follow-ups revealed that the child experienced no complications, infections, or recurrence of the lipoma. The reconstructed nasal septum exhibited excellent structural integrity and aesthetic alignment, reaffirming the success of the surgical intervention.

The implications of this case extend beyond the immediate success of the procedure. This innovative approach offers a promising avenue for treating similar rare conditions and presents an opportunity for further research into its broader applications in head and neck surgery.

### A Comprehensive Review of Literature

In addition to reporting this groundbreaking case, the surgical team conducted a thorough review of the existing literature on nasal septal lipomas. With only 16 cases documented prior to this one, the review highlights how little is known about this rare condition, including its etiology, clinical presentation, and optimal treatment methods.

The review also underscores the importance of reporting and analyzing rare cases to expand the collective understanding of uncommon medical phenomena. Each documented case contributes valuable insights that can inform future diagnoses and treatments.

### Paving the Way for Future Innovations

The successful management of this rare nasal septal lipoma serves as a testament to the importance of surgical ingenuity and interdisciplinary collaboration in the field of otolaryngology. By introducing a novel reconstruction technique, this case not only adds to the scant body of literature on nasal septal lipomas but also establishes a new benchmark for treating similar anomalies.

While nasal septal lipomas remain rare, the lessons learned from this case could have far-reaching implications, inspiring surgeons to explore innovative approaches to challenging conditions. As medicine continues to evolve, cases like this remind us of the power of creative problem-solving and its ability to turn obstacles into opportunities.

This rare case report will undoubtedly serve as a cornerstone for future research and clinical practice, providing hope for patients with similarly rare conditions and demonstrating the boundless possibilities of modern medicine.

articles.title: 2nd quarter sees decline in new HIV cases
articles.summary: THE second quarter of 2025 saw a slight decline in the number of new cases of the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), based on data released by the...
articles.content:
**2nd Quarter Sees Decline in New HIV Cases**  
*Encouraging Trend Highlights Progress in Public Health Initiatives*  

In a promising development for public health, the second quarter of 2025 recorded a slight decline in new cases of the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), according to data released this week by the National Health Monitoring Agency (NHMA). The report, which tracks HIV statistics across the country, offers a glimmer of hope in the ongoing battle against the virus that has been a global health challenge for decades.  

The NHMA report revealed that new HIV diagnoses dropped by 3.7% compared to the same period in 2024. While the decline may appear modest, experts are hailing the trend as a sign that sustained prevention and treatment efforts are beginning to bear fruit.  

### **A Closer Look at the Numbers**  
From April to June 2025, 8,750 new HIV cases were reported, down from 9,090 cases during the same quarter last year. The decline was observed across several demographic groups, though some regions and populations continue to face higher risks.  

Urban centers, which have historically recorded higher infection rates, saw significant progress, with many cities reporting declines of 5% or more. However, rural areas showed less pronounced improvements, underscoring the need for targeted interventions in these communities.  

The NHMA report also highlighted a reduction in the number of cases among young adults aged 20–29, a group that has been a focal point for awareness campaigns in recent years. Despite this progress, challenges remain, particularly among marginalized communities who face systemic barriers to accessing healthcare and education about the virus.  

### **The Role of Prevention Programs**  
Health officials attribute the decline to a combination of factors, including expanded access to pre-exposure prophylaxis (PrEP), more widespread HIV testing, and robust public education campaigns. Organizations at the local and national levels have worked tirelessly to destigmatize conversations around HIV and to promote the use of preventive measures.  

“The progress we’re seeing is the result of years of coordinated effort,” said Dr. Elaine Matthews, an epidemiologist with the NHMA. “We’ve been able to reach more people with lifesaving information and resources, and that’s making a real difference.”  

One standout initiative has been the "Know Your Status" campaign, which has focused on increasing the number of people who get tested for HIV. Free and confidential testing services have been made more accessible, particularly in hard-to-reach areas, through mobile clinics and pop-up sites.  

### **Challenges Ahead**  
Despite the encouraging numbers, experts caution that the fight against HIV is far from over. The virus continues to disproportionately affect vulnerable populations, including LGBTQ+ communities, people who inject drugs, and individuals in low-income regions.  

Stigma and misinformation remain significant barriers. “Even with all the advancements in treatment and prevention, we’re still seeing people avoid testing due to fear of discrimination,” said Carlos Rivera, a community health advocate. “That’s something we have to address if we want to see even greater declines.”  

The NHMA report also noted a slight uptick in cases among those over 50, a demographic that has often been overlooked in prevention campaigns. Health officials are now calling for more inclusive messaging that reaches all age groups.  

### **Looking Ahead**  
The decline in new HIV cases is undoubtedly good news, but maintaining this momentum will require sustained commitment from policymakers, healthcare providers, and communities. Advocates are urging continued investment in preventive measures, improved access to antiretroviral therapy, and comprehensive sex education.  

“We should celebrate this progress, but we can’t afford to become complacent,” said Dr. Matthews. “Every new case is one too many. Our goal has to be zero new infections.”  

As the world marks this small but meaningful victory, the hope is that the lessons learned from the successes of the past year will pave the way for even greater achievements in the fight against HIV. For now, the data offers a reminder that progress—however incremental—is possible with dedication, innovation, and compassion.
